description_long: This is a two-story brick house with a dark, hipped roof and a prominent covered porch at the front, supported by columns and accessed by a set of steps. The exterior walls are clad in light brown brick, and the house sits on a rectangular plot with a concrete or stone foundation. Windows are arranged in an asymmetrical pattern, with a mix of single and double units, and a chimney is visible on one side of the roof. The rear of the house features a covered patio or veranda, partially enclosed with railings, providing an outdoor living space. The roof has multiple slopes and overhangs, giving the structure a substantial and traditional appearance. The overall design suggests a blend of Craftsman and modern suburban architectural influences.
description: A two-story brick house with a dark hipped roof, covered front porch, and asymmetrical window arrangement. Features include a chimney, rear veranda, and traditional architectural details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway with a covered porch, steps, and columns.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the chimney, asymmetrical window placement, and the rear covered veranda.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the covered veranda and back entrance, with visible windows and roof overhang.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing additional windows and the extended roofline over the veranda.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with steps, columns, and a prominent overhang
